user_message:
Read-only review pass — return text only, no file edits. <task> Repo: /Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext Plan file: /Users/teslamint/.claude/plans/robust-humming-papert.md This is round 4 of an iterative plan review for GitHub issue #41 (milestone v0.2.0). Previous round raised: A.B8 (partial), B1, B3, B5, B9a, B9b as Important items. The plan was edited to address all five. Verify only the new edits. Ground truth files (read these to verify plan claims): - src/entirecontext/db/schema.py lines 65-116 - src/entirecontext/core/futures.py and src/entirecontext/cli/futures_cmds.py (staged-diff assessment claim) - tests/test_decisions_cli.py lines 466-580 - src/entirecontext/hooks/decision_hooks.py lines 712-774 - src/entirecontext/core/telemetry.py lines 200-265 Read the following plan sections: - The Context bullet list - Scope → Explicitly out of scope bullet about staged-diff assessments - Source-specific signal logic table AND the new Turn window definition subsection - Telemetry severity mapping paragraph inside Pipeline Architecture - Backwards compatibility and test migration section including Shim call path pseudo-code and updated test mapping table </task> <structured_output_contract> Return two sections exactly: Section A — For each of A.B8, B1, B3, B5, B9a, B9b: one line per item with status RESOLVED / PARTIALLY RESOLVED / NOT RESOLVED and a one-sentence reason grounded in the plan text or repo file. Section B — New issues only (not pre-existing). Check these seven specific questions, numbered 1-7: 1. Turn window definition SQL uses timestamp <= :C_created_at. Should it be strict < to avoid double-counting when a checkpoint is created in the same tick as the last turn? 2. The Shim call path pseudo-code calls ex.mark_session_extracted(...). Is that helper defined in the new core module: core/decision_extraction.py public API block in the plan? If not, flag as a silent assumption. 3. Shim marks session extracted on empty bundles (no signals found). Is that intentional or does it prevent future pipeline improvements from rediscovering those sessions? 4. The telemetry severity paragraph says background worker swallows unexpected errors. Does that conflict with hooks/session_lifecycle.py:_maybe_extract_decisions pattern? Confirm consistent or flag conflict. 5. The staged-diff exclusion note says assessments without checkpoint_id are invisible to both the pre-launch gate JOIN and the collector. Does the collector SQL in the plan use the same JOIN shape as the gate, or does one use LEFT JOIN / OR branch that would include them? 6. test_extract_from_session_idempotent seeds decisions_extracted=True and plan says add a twin seeding candidates_extracted=True. Is the v12 shims treat old marker as satisfying new behavior covered by a dedicated assertion, or only implicitly?

7. Any other new issues in the edits.

Severity: Critical / Important / Suggestion. Cite plan section or file:line.
If Section A is fully resolved and Section B has no new findings, say No new findings, plan is ready explicitly.
Target 600-900 words total.
</structured_output_contract>

<grounding_rules>
- Every finding must cite a specific plan section or file:line from the repo files listed above.
- Label hypotheses clearly. Do not present inferences as facts.
- Only submit a finding if you can point to a specific code path or plan text that causes the issue.
- Do not flag pre-existing issues from earlier rounds unless the new edit made them worse.
- Do not flag style preferences not codified in the project CLAUDE.md.
</grounding_rules>

<default_follow_through_policy>
Do not edit any files. Return text feedback only.
</default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: 요청 범위는 새로 수정된 계획 항목만 검증하는 읽기 전용 리뷰로 이해했습니다. 먼저 `code-review` 스킬 지침과 EntireContext의 관련 결정/레슨을 빠르게 확인한 뒤, 지정된 plan 섹션과 근거 파일만 대조해 A/B 형식으로 정리하겠습니다.
